value: Commander.js CLI. Core CRUD: set/s, get/g, run/r, find/f, edit/e, copy/cp, rename/rn, remove/rm. Subcommand groups: alias (set/remove/list/rename), confirm (set/remove/list), config, data. Top-level: context, info, stale, lint, stats, audit. Hidden: search (alias for find). Inline alias shortcut: `set <key> <value> -a <name>` is the documented one-shot alias creation path (README line 338); equivalent to running `alias set` after. Filter shorts: `find -a` (aliases-only) and `find -e` (entries-only) are documented search filters. Legacy `-a` flags on get/remove/rename are undocumented entry points to the alias subcommand functionality — README only shows the `alias <subcommand>` form for those operations, so consider those redundant rather than canonical. Deprecated: --raw on get and context (renamed to --plain in v1.9.1, old flag still works).
